title: M3: TLS/TCPS + ewallet.pem + tnsnames/IFILE + connect-string corpus differential (anti-fake-parity); cwallet.sso value-add

M5: Arrow dataframes (arrow-rs RecordBatch + C Data Interface shim export) + direct path load (TTC 128/129/130) — modules 8000-9400,9600,9700 match-or-beat baseline; upstream-WIP bugs fixed not reproduced (Q7)
Implement fetch-var define metadata for outputtypehandler LOB-to-string REF CURSOR fetches
notes:
Discovered from rust-oracledb-bfy while reducing test_1300_cursor_var.py to one failing test.

Current failure: test_1309 opens a REF CURSOR over a CLOB and installs an outputtypehandler returning cursor.var(oracledb.LONG_STRING, arraysize=cursor.arraysize). Fetching should produce the original string value. The Rust shim currently has no fetch-var/define metadata path for outputtypehandler results, so the execute/fetch protocol leaves the column as a CLOB locator and query conversion fails with unsupported query column type.

Acceptance evidence needed:
- Implement outputtypehandler-driven fetch vars/define metadata for sync REF CURSOR fetch.
- reference/python-oracledb/tests/test_1300_cursor_var.py fully green.
- Preserve reference/python-oracledb/tests/test_3900_cursor_execute.py green.
- cargo fmt/check/build/test plus ubs on changed Rust files.
close_reason: Implemented outputtypehandler fetch-var define metadata for REF CURSOR LOB-to-string fetches; test_1300_cursor_var.py and test_3900_cursor_execute.py are green.

title: M6: Gauntlet certification — filtered 72-module suite matches-or-beats baseline, fuzz clean, fake-parity sweep, perf vs python-oracledb+rust-oracle incl. bulk-load
Complete cursor-var REF CURSOR and cursor-valued column semantics
notes:
Cursor-var checkpoint after REF CURSOR bind and cursor-valued column slice.

Implemented in this slice:
- Public cursor values can be validated and bound as REF CURSOR inputs, including self-bind, closed-cursor, and cross-connection error paths.
- OUT cursor variables hydrate returned cursor metadata and mark zero cursor ids as invalid REF CURSORs.
- Cursor-valued query columns are converted into public child cursors via the owning connection cursor factory, preserving custom cursor classes.
- Fetch metadata now reports DB_TYPE_CURSOR for cursor columns and DB_TYPE_CHAR for CHAR metadata.

Current live evidence against local gvenzl/oracle-free with the current PyO3 shim installed into .venv-py313:
- reference/python-oracledb/tests/test_1300_cursor_var.py: 20 passed, 1 failed (test_1309).
- reference/python-oracledb/tests/test_3900_cursor_execute.py: 37 passed.
- cargo build -p oracledb-pyshim: passed.
- cargo test -p oracledb-protocol -p oracledb -p oracledb-pyshim: passed.
- cargo fmt --all --check: passed.
- git diff --check: passed.
- ubs crates/oracledb-protocol/src/thin.rs crates/oracledb-pyshim/src/lib.rs: exit 0, 0 critical issues.

Remaining gap: test_1309 fails while fetching a REF CURSOR whose CLOB column should be converted by outputtypehandler to DB_TYPE_LONG/string. The Rust shim does not yet send fetch-var define metadata, so Oracle returns locator semantics and the query value conversion path rejects the column type. Follow-up tracked in rust-oracledb-1yy. Keep this Bead in_progress until that gap is implemented and the full module is green.
close_reason: Completed cursor-var REF CURSOR semantics; full python-oracledb test_1300_cursor_var.py passes through the shim.

title: Implement object type metadata gettype support for FetchInfo/object columns
close_reason: Implemented gettype-backed object metadata for FetchInfo/object columns; test_3900_cursor_execute.py is green

title: M4: DbObject/XMLType + OSON/JSON + VECTOR + pool/DRCP + pipelining + sessionless txn + all async modules
M2: execute/fetch/binds + core scalar types + LOB/LONG — suite 1000-5200 series matches baseline
notes:
M2 checkpoint after completing cursor-var REF CURSOR semantics and outputtypehandler fetch-var define metadata.

Implemented in recent M2 work:
- Scalar OUT binds, DML RETURNING, and REF CURSOR callfunc/callproc paths route through the Rust protocol/shim path.
- Protocol execute distinguishes PL/SQL OUT binds from DML RETURNING metadata-only outputs, uses AL32UTF8 worst-case text bind sizing, and preserves REF CURSOR sequence behavior.
- ThinCursorImpl hydrates returned REF CURSOR metadata, converts cursor-valued query columns into public child cursors, and marks unopened REF CURSOR values invalid.
- FetchInfo/object metadata supports conn.gettype("UDT_OBJECT"), DB_TYPE_OBJECT type_code, object objtype equality, and object column describe metadata.
- Outputtypehandler fetch vars now drive define metadata for REF CURSOR fetches, including CLOB-to-string via LONG define metadata.

Current live evidence against local gvenzl/oracle-free with the current PyO3 shim installed into .venv-py313:
- reference/python-oracledb/tests/test_1300_cursor_var.py: 21 passed.
- reference/python-oracledb/tests/test_3900_cursor_execute.py: 37 passed.
- cargo test -p oracledb-protocol -p oracledb -p oracledb-pyshim: passed.
- cargo fmt --all --check: passed.
- git diff --check: passed.
- ubs crates/oracledb-protocol/src/thin.rs crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs crates/oracledb-pyshim/src/lib.rs: exit 0, 0 critical issues.

Closed follow-ups: rust-oracledb-d9w, rust-oracledb-bfy, rust-oracledb-1yy. M2 remains in_progress for the broader 1000-5200 series beyond these focused modules.

title: M1: Connect + 12c/11g auth + identity masquerade gate (v$session reflects caller-set osuser/program/machine) + test_1100 green
notes: M1 progress: Rust thin CONNECT/ACCEPT, fast auth phase one/two, 12c verifier, marker reset, server piggyback skip, ping, close are live-green against localhost:1522/FREEPDB1. PyO3 ThinConnImpl now bridges public oracledb.connect() to Rust for connect attributes, ping, close using harness env password. Identity gate verified via reference query: program/machine/terminal/osuser/client_driver matched Rust-set values. Remaining M1 gate blocker: python-oracledb test_1100 setup calls conn.cursor() for env initialization; ThinConnImpl.create_cursor_impl is still an explicit placeholder, so execute/fetch cursor implementation is next.
close_reason: Completed M1: Rust thin auth/connect, execute/fetch text cursor, identity gates test_1146-test_1150, and python-oracledb test_1100 pass through PyO3 shim
